name: The Eternal Watch
text: Action: During a conflict at this province, choose an attacking character. Your opponent selects one – either bow that character or they give you 1 honor.

name: Seeking Enlightenment
text: Reaction: After this province is revealed – your opponent loses X fate, where X is the number of attacking characters.

name: By Onnotangu's Light
text: During conflicts at this province, each character in play is considered to have no fate on it, and fate tokens cannot be moved or removed from those characters.

name: Damned Hida
text: Dire – This character gets +3[mil]. (This character is dire when it has no fate on it.)

name: Desperate Negotiator
text: Dire – This character gets +2[mil] and +2[pol]. (This character is dire when it has no fate on it.)

name: Togashi Kazue
text: Each character you control gains: "Dire – You may trigger each of this character's triggered abilities an additional time each round (or specified period)."

name: Lion's Pride Paragon
text: Dire – This character does not bow as a result of conflict resolution. (This character is dire when it has no fate on it.)

name: Forward Garrison
text: While there is a Battlefield in play, fate cannot be removed from characters you control by opponents' card or ring effects.

name: Kaito Mai
text:
Dire – This character gets +3 glory.
Reaction: After 1 or more fate is removed from this character outside the fate phase, choose another character – remove 1 fate from that character.

name: Yogo Paramour
text: Dire – This character gains: "Action: Bow this character. Choose another character – dishonor that character.". (This character is dire when it has no fate on it.)

name: Shadowed Village
text: Reaction: After 1 or more fate is removed from a character you control outside the fate phase – draw 1 card (draw 2 cards instead if that character is dishonored)

name: Iuchi Shahai
text:
While you are the first player, this character gains covert.
Reaction: After you play a Meishōdō or Mahō card, lose 1 honor – place 1 fate on this character.

name: Resourceful Mahō-Tsukai
text:
No attachments except Shadowlands or Spell.
While this character is dishonored, reduce the cost to play each Mahō card you play by 1 (to a minimum of 0).

name: Tetsubō of Blood
text:
This card's fate cost can only be paid for with fate on characters you control.
Limited. Restricted.
Attached character cannot be honored.

name: Disparaging Challenge
text: Action: During a conflict, initiate a [pol] duel against a character your opponent controls in their home area – resolve the duel. Move the duel's loser to the conflict, if able. Otherwise, move it home.

name: Ki Alignment
text: Reaction: After you declare a Monk character as an attacker or defender – search the top 8 cards of your conflict deck for up to 2 Kihō cards with different names, reveal them, and add them to your hand. Shuffle.

name: Feeding An Army
text:
Air role only.
Reaction: After the conflict phase begins, break a faceup (friendly) province – place 1 fate on each character you control with printed cost 3 or lower.

name: In Harmony
text:
Play only if you have at least 1 ring in your claimed ring pool.
Ancestral.
Fate on attached character cannot be removed by card or ring effects.

name: Sanguine Mastery
text:
Fire role only.
This card's fate cost can only be paid for with fate on characters you control.
Action: Dishonor a (friendly) character. Choose up to X attachments, where X is the dishonored character's glory – discard each of those attachments.

name: Seize the Mind
text:
This card's fate cost can only be paid for with fate on characters you control.
Action: During a conflict, choose a non-unique character – take control of that character until the end of the conflict. You lose honor equal to the amount of fate on that character.

name: Command the Tributary
text: Attached character gains: "Action: Choose another character – move 1 fate from this character to the chosen character."

name: Bonds of Blood (card)
text:
This card's fate cost can only be paid for with fate on characters you control.
Action: During a conflict, dishonor a (friendly) participating character. Choose a character – move both the dishonored character and the chosen character home.
